methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $3.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $5.8B - $312.5B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Palantir
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $312.5B

private.name: Crusoe Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $10B+

private.name: SandboxAQ
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $5.75B

data.narrative:
Ayar Labs, a Series E AI & Software company founded by Mark Wade and Vladimir Stojanovic, is evaluated against a comp universe of 9 peers—1 public (Palantir) and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage.

Two valuation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis takes the last post-money valuation of $3.8B and applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical for Series E advancement, yielding $5.2B. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables: Palantir at $312.5B, Crusoe Energy at $10.0B, and SandboxAQ at $5.8B. The median of this set is $10.0B, though the range reflects extreme dispersion, with Palantir as an outlier.

Triangulation across both methods produces a valuation range of $4.5B (low) to $11.5B (high), with a median of $7.6B. The current post-money valuation of $3.75B sits below this estimated range. Key uncertainties include the outsized influence of Palantir in the peer set—a mature, publicly-traded company—which distorts median comparability for an earlier-stage private firm. The tightness of the stage-progression method depends on whether 1.4x reflects true market conditions for this cohort. Capital raised ($870M+) supports the valuation envelope but provides limited insight into forward exit potential or revenue multiples.
